meaning: n.遮阳帽,[动](非洲的)转角牛羚
examples: The later species all depend on the preparations of the earlier one, for the actions of the zebra alter the vegetation to suit the stomachs of the wildebeest, topi, and gazelle.
The other three species differ in the proportion of lower leaves and higher stems that they eat: zebras eat the most stem matter, wildebeests eat the most leaves, and topi are intermediate.
Thus, when there is only a short supply of poor-quality food, the wildebeest, topi, and gazelle enjoy an advantage.
Numbers vary annually, but it is estimated that herd sizes can reach 1.5 million wildebeest, not to mention just under a million zebras, topi and eland.
While group chat apps like Groupme are popular at events, Topi is designed to create groups specifically to make it easier to meet people and connect at conferences.
